channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: 22 Belsize Park, 11th April
item.description:
Pavement has been completely butchered and is now at a steep incline which prevents wheelchair users from using the pavement safely. How on earth was this accepted by the council?

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 9, Belsize Park, Belsize Park, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Fridge, 11th April
item.description:
Fridge freezer on road
Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): William Barefoot Drive, Eltham
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Road sign, 11th April
item.description:
Due to car crash, road sign is now gone

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Birch Grove, Blackfen, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: The door to communal bin area needs jet washing/ filthy for a long time, 11th April
item.description:
Rudbeck House, Flats 9-16 The door to the waste bin area is absolutely filthy with some sort of residue. It is on the inside of the door. It needs proper jet washing and scrubbing off the dirt as it has been left like this for a very long time and it has dried out. Please assist

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Peckham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: In the garden of John Barker court, which is a Brent council owned block of flats, 11th April
item.description:
Please come and remove it please. It has been there for over a week.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Brondesbury Park, Kilburn, London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Fly-tip Large - More than a van load (Estates)

item.title: Unnecessary traffic control barriers, 11th April
item.description:
Woodvale was recently resurfaced with speed bumps which was great. But now the council has decided to put temporary artificially windy traffic control barriers over the top which is causing the drive to be unpleasantly windy, limits the number of cars that can be parked along the street, and makes it dangerous with buses also using the road. The reduction in the number of cars that can now be parked along that street means that cars are now flowing over to our much smaller street (Melford Road). The temporary barriers are causing havoc to the rest of the neighbourhood and is completely unnecessary. Why has the council decided to do this?? Money should instead be used to resurface Melford Rd which has a million potholes on it and is in constant use by double decker buses
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Wood Vale, Forest Hill, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Opposite 59 Malvern road, 11th April
item.description:
Dumped washing machine been here for a number of days

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Stafford Close, London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Fly-tip Small - Less than one bag (Estates)

item.title: A box of flytipped clothing, 11th April
item.description:
Straight after the council came and cleared the previous flytipped pile of rubbish, a new pile has been put in the same place.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Brabourn Grove, Nunhead, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Malvern road, 11th April
item.description:
Malvern road is again in desperate need of street sweeping. Since Yuri stopped working on Malvern road it has become a real mess and is again being neglected by your street sweepers.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Stafford Close, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.description:
Abadoned sofa

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 49,51, Walsingham Road, St Paul's Cray, Greater London

item.title: Fly-tipping, 11th April
item.description:
Opposite No.35 Watford Rd

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Walford Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Pothole, 11th April
item.description:
Huge area pothole in the middle of the road. Seems the council reply is to “monitor “ it. Can you tell me at what point the council will actually do something to repair???? Is it going to need damage to a car or injury to a person on a bike before that happens? Please let me know
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 99, Halfway Street, Longlands, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.description:
A fridge has been fly tipped near 92 Anerley Road

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 90-92, Anerley Road, Crystal Palace, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Large chair dumped on footpath at corner of st Martin's and Burnley Road, 11th April
item.description:
Not sure how long this chair has been here but it is partially obstruction an already narrow footpath.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 14-25, Burnley Road, Stockwell, Greater London

item.title: Cardboard boxes everywhere hillbeck close se15 1bt, 11th April
item.description:
Looks terrible and will attract more rubbish dumping
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Hillbeck Close, Peckham, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Consistent litterer, 11th April
item.description:
The driver/owner of this private hire vehicle is consistently dumping their rubbish around and behind the phone cabinets. Residents of Stodart Road clean up after this person, but we would appreciate enforcement action being taken.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 39, Stodart Road, Anerley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
